item.title: Hormuz shutdown worsens after US hits Iranian warship
item.description: The US–Iran war widened on Wednesday after a US strike hit an Iranian warship off Sri Lanka, deepening a crisis that has paralysed shipping through the Strait of Hormuz for a fifth day and choked off vital Middle East oil and gas flows. The US submarine strike on the Iranian vessel came as U

item.title: UCB observes Financial Literacy Day with nationwide program
item.description: United Commercial Bank PLC (UCB) organised a special nationwide program on the first Monday of March to mark Financial Literacy Day, according to a press release. The program was held simultaneously at the bank’s Head Office and at branches across all divisions of the country. Through this i

item.title: Reshuffle in police admin: 10 SPs transferred
item.description: In a latest reshuffle in police administration, ten top officials of Bangladesh police under superintendent of police rank have been transferred and given new postings. Ministry of Home Affairs issued a notification in this regard on Wednesday. According to the notification, Md Shahadat Hossain s

item.title: Man sent to jail after confessing to killing 8-year-old girl in Sitakunda
item.description: A man accused of killing an 8-year-old girl in Sitakunda has confessed in court to his involvement in the crime. Babu Sheikh, the suspect, gave his statement on Wednesday before Judicial Magistrate-1 Sayma Afrin Hima, admitting his guilt in the killing of Jannatul Nesa, also known as Ira Moni, acc

item.title: Ministry, BCB jointly working to return Shakib to field, says Asif Akbar
item.description: Bangladesh Cricket Board (BCB) Director Asif Akbar has said the sports ministry and the board are working jointly to facilitate Shakib Al Hasan’s return to cricket. On Wednesday, Asif, chairman of the Age-Based Tournament Committee, said the legal issues that had created complications around

item.title: At least 80 killed in US strike on Iranian warship, Sri Lankan minister says
item.description: At least ​80 were ‌people killed ​in a ​US submarine strike ⁠on ​an Iranian ​warship in the ​Indian ​Ocean, Sri Lanka's ‌deputy ⁠foreign minister told local ​television ​on ⁠Wednesday.

item.title: US trade deal discussed with BNP, Jamaat before signing: FM
item.description: Foreign Minister Dr Khalilur Rahman on Wednesday rejected claims that the reciprocal trade agreement between Bangladesh and the US was signed abruptly just days before the recently held national election. He said the matter had been discussed in advance with the leadership of the country’s t

item.title: Open Mic to amplify public voices for women’s and girls’ rights
item.description: As part of global campaign to mark the International Women’s Day, the United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA) in Bangladesh listened to common people in an Open Mic event at Shahabuddin Park in Gulshan on Wednesday. The Open Mic event forms part of UNFPA Bangladesh’s broader Internation

item.title: US says Iran firing fewer missiles, US strikes to expand inland
item.description: Iran is firing fewer missiles than it did at ‌the start of the war on Saturday, the top U.S. general said on Wednesday, as he argued Iran’s ​military capabilities were greatly diminished as the ​United States expands its strikes inside Iran. “We will ⁠now begin to expand inland st

item.title: Govt moving to restore discipline in public hospitals: Health minister
item.description: Health and Family Welfare Minister Sardar Md Sakhawat Hossain on Wednesday said the government will take firm and effective measures to stop irregularities in public hospitals and restore discipline in the healthcare system. He made the remarks during a surprise visit to Dhaka Medical College Hosp

item.title: IFIC Bank signs MoU with Cumilla City Corporation
item.description: IFIC Bank PLC has entered a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with Cumilla City Corporation with a view to enhancing citizen services, improving efficiency in service delivery, and extending modern banking services to the doorstep of the people, according to a press release. The MoU was signed on

item.title: Canada's services PMI shows activity declining further in February
item.description: Canada's services economy contracted for a fourth straight month in February as an uncertain trading environment contributed to declines in ​activity and new business, S&P Global's Canada services PMI data ‌showed on Wednesday. The headline Business Activity Index edged up to 46.5 last month
